disclaimer: Educational simulation only — not medical advice. Outcomes are modeled indices for learning.

cases.name: Mr. K — IDH
cases.story: Case 1 · Hemodynamic. Diabetic, 3×/week in-center HD via AVF. Large interdialytic weight gain (+3.8 kg); UF goal 3.5 L over 4 h. Took morning antihypertensive. 90 min in: lightheadedness, yawning, left calf cramp.
learning_objectives: Recognise the plasma-refill vs UF-rate mechanism of IDH
Execute the stop-UF / Trendelenburg / measured-bolus first response
Build the multifactorial prevention bundle (UF rate, dry weight, AM antihypertensive hold, cool dialysate)
Explain how convective HDF reduces IDH incidence (CONVINCE secondary endpoint)

cases.name: Mrs. R — Arrhythmia
cases.story: Case 2 · Cardiac [preview — full case in production build]. Ischemic heart disease + LVH, on digoxin. Predialysis K⁺ 4.6 mmol/L (low-normal). Dialysate K⁺ mis-set to 1.0 mmol/L. Two hours in: palpitations, then ventricular ectopy.
learning_objectives: Identify the low-dialysate-K trap in digitalis patients
Correct dialysate K to 3–3.5 mEq/L as first-line fix
Understand the sudden-death risk of K < 2 mmol/L in at-risk patients (Karaboyas 2017 AJKD)

cases.name: Mr. T — Hyperkalemia
cases.story: Case 3 · Electrolytes [preview — full case in production build]. Missed two sessions, large K⁺ intake. Unscheduled: weak, palpitations, paresthesias. Point-of-care K⁺ 7.2 mmol/L, tented T waves, QRS beginning to widen.
learning_objectives: Apply the three-level order: stabilise (IV Ca) → shift (insulin/albuterol) → remove (HD)
Understand why calcium must precede dialysis when QRS is widening
State HD K⁺ removal capacity (70–150 mEq per session)

cases.name: Cluster — Concentrate Error
cases.story: Case 4 · Acid–base [preview — full case in production build]. Mid-session: three patients on one water loop report dyspnoea, nausea, and air hunger within 20 min. New acid concentrate connected that morning; one machine's conductivity reads borderline.
learning_objectives: Recognise symptom clusters on a shared loop as a machine/concentrate signal, not individual patient events
Apply the immediate response: stop treatments, check conductivity + concentrate connections, draw blood gas
Articulate interlocks staff must trust to prevent accidental acidosis
